title: Count Pairs With Given Sum — O(n)
description:
Given an integer array and a target value k, return the count of (unordered) pairs (i, j) with i < j such that nums[i] + nums[j] = k.

**The point**: nested loops give O(n²). A single pass with a Map of seen values + counts solves it in **O(n)**.

examples.explanation: Every pair of indices sums to 2 — that's C(4,2)=6 pairs.

hints: Nested loop: for each i, scan all j > i. That's O(n²).
Walk left to right. For each new value x, ask: 'how many previous values equal k - x?'
A Map of value → seen count makes that question O(1). Update the map AFTER counting.
